source.definition: A single part of a series, especially a television or radio program installment.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "episode" comes from the Greek word "epísodos," meaning 'entrance' or 'incident,' originally referring to a part of a play or story.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „episode” provine din cuvântul grecesc „epísodos”, care înseamnă „intrare” sau „eveniment”, referindu-se inițial la o parte a unei piese sau povești.

target.definition: O parte individuală a unei serii, în special a unui program de televiziune sau radio.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "episod" was borrowed from the French "épisode," which in turn comes from the Greek "epísodos" ("entrance, intermediate event").
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „episod” a fost preluat din francezul „épisode”, care provine din grecescul „epísodos” („intrare, eveniment intermediar”).

examples.sourceText: The new episode of the series will be released next week.
examples.targetText: Noul episod al serialului apare săptămâna viitoare.

examples.sourceText: I did not watch the last episode.

examples.targetText: Fiecare episod durează aproximativ 45 de minute.

examples.sourceText: The episode was broadcast on television.
examples.targetText: Episodul a fost difuzat la televizor.

learningTips.text: Remember 'episode' as a part of a story, like a chapter in a book or a scene in a TV show.
The Romanian 'episod' sounds very similar to the English 'episode,' making it easy to recall.
Think of 'episode' as an 'event' or 'incident' within a larger narrative, which helps connect to its Greek origin.
Imagine a TV series as a chain of events—each 'episode' is a link in that chain.
